context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the testing-essentials instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill before writing ANY `_test.exs` file.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `async: true` only when safe** — safe: pure functions, changesets, helpers; unsafe: DB contexts with shared rows, LiveView, `Application.put_env`, external services

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Define test data in fixtures** (`test/support/`) — never build it inline across multiple tests

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `has_element?/2` and `element/2` for LiveView assertions** — not `html =~ "text"` for structure checks

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always test the unauthorized case** for any protected resource

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never hardcode dates** — use relative timestamps to prevent flaky tests

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Check existing fixtures** — inspect `test/support/fixtures/` for relevant fixtures before creating new ones

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Create fixture if needed** — add to the appropriate fixtures module (see Fixture Pattern below)

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify compilation** — run `mix test` to confirm the fixture compiles before writing any tests

checklist.name: instruction-10
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Write the failing test** — implement the test case; run `mix test path/to/file_test.exs` and confirm it fails with a meaningful message (not a compile error)
